message:
test(fcp-core): pin DecisionReceipt 7-field serde matrix + Decision-tag wire form (flywheel_connectors-hw4hi)

Bead asks for RoutingDecision JSON+CBOR roundtrip + serde tag pinning.
No type literally named RoutingDecision exists in fcp-core; the closest
serializable decision-record is DecisionReceipt at audit.rs:317 — the
"why allowed/denied" record returned by the policy engine that powers
fcp explain. PolicyDecision at policy.rs:2196 is a runtime struct with
no serde derives; DecisionReceipt is the on-the-wire form.

Existing audit_chain_golden_vectors.rs covers DecisionReceipt
construction + is_allow/is_deny predicates. policy_decision_serde_tag_matrix.rs
covers the bare Decision enum. This pin adds the residual full-struct
serde matrix.

Coverage: 7-field full + 6-field minimal JSON shape, skip-when-None for
explanation, embedded Decision lowercase serde tag inside the receipt,
empty evidence Vec serializes as [] (no skip_serializing_if — pin so
future skip-when-empty silently dropping evidence is caught loudly,
since no-evidence is a different audit statement from missing-evidence-
field), JSON+CBOR full round-trip preserves decision/reason_code/
evidence/explanation, JSON+CBOR cross-format equality, distinct-decision
sentinel, distinct-reason-code sentinel, distinct-evidence-list
sentinel (security-critical field), is_allow/is_deny survives JSON
round-trip on both polarities, PascalCase Decision rejection through
the embedded receipt deserialize path.

14 tests green via CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p1-hw4hi cargo test -p
fcp-core --test routing_decision_serde_tag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
